plan_metadata.name: Sample Graph RSC Helper Debug in Group Chat

steps.description: Type text: 'Graph RSC Helper' into the search input field located within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Create" button for the "Graph RSC Helper" sample in the "Samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the default folder path in the "Workspace Folder" dialog box within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'GRAPH-RSC-HELPER' is visible
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while viewing the Explorer panel.
Type the text 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette, accessed via the top input field with the '>' prefix.
Press the "Enter" key to select the highlighted command "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" within the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.

parameters.text: Debug in group chat for RSC permissions (Chrome)

steps.description: Type text 'Debug in group chat for RSC permissions (Chrome)' into the launch configuration search input field in the Visual Studio Code command palette to select the corresponding debug configuration option.

Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the debug configuration item "Debug in group chat for RSC permissions (Chrome)" from the dropdown in the Visual Studio Code debug configurations menu.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft login page interface to focus the cursor and enable entry of user credentials.
Click the "Yes" button on the "Stay signed in?" prompt of the Microsoft login interface at coordinates (631, 530) to confirm staying signed in.
Click the "Add" button located beneath the "GraphAPIExplorerforRSC" app's title in the Microsoft Teams app interface, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (302, 217).
Click the red "+" icon in the Chrome tab bar to open a new tab in the browser interface.
Type the URL 'https://localhost:3978/configure' into the Chrome browser's address bar on the 'New Tab' page.
Press the "Enter" key to navigate to the URL "https://localhost:3978/configure" from the browser's address bar in Google Chrome.
Click the "Advanced" button on the Chrome privacy error page to proceed with advanced settings or options when the connection is flagged as not private.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link to bypass the security warning on the browser's "Privacy error" page.
Click the browser tab labeled "Chat - Long Hao | Mi..." to switch to the desired application interface.
Click on the "Long Hao" chat entry in the "Select a chat" list within the "GraphAPIExplorerforRSC" success dialog in Microsoft Teams.
Click the "Continue" button at the bottom of the "GraphAPIExplorerforRSC - Added successfully!" prompt to proceed after selecting a channel.
Click the "Allow" button in the permissions prompt titled "teams.microsoft.com wants to" to enable the site to look for and connect to devices on your local network.
Click the "Save" button located at the bottom-right corner of the "GraphAPIExplorerforRSC" modal dialog within the Microsoft Teams web application.
@assertion the UI is displayed correctly and 'Graph API Category with RSC Permissions' is visible,
Use keyboard shortcut: Ctrl + - to zoom out the view within the Microsoft Teams interface, where the Graph API Explorer is displayed with RSC permissions details.
Use keyboard shortcut `Ctrl+-` to zoom out in the Microsoft Teams desktop application interface, adjusting the view of the Graph API configuration page within the "Chat" section.
Click on the dropdown labeled "Chat Member" under the "Graph API Category with RSC Permissions" section in the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click the "GET" button under the "List Members" section in the "Chat Member" dropdown, located within the "Graph API Category with RSC Permissions" panel in Microsoft Teams.
Click the "Submit" button on the Graph API interface to initiate the API request at the specified endpoint URL.
Click the side bar to drop down to the end of the browser
@assertion the response contains valid data, no error returns. And "email": "haolong@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com" is visible
